segments.text: This is what the LORD says:

Maintain justice and do what is right, for My salvation is coming soon, 
and My righteousness will be revealed.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Blessed is the man who does this, and the son of man who holds it fast, who keeps the Sabbath without profaning it and keeps his hand from doing any evil.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Let no foreigner who has joined himself to the LORD say,
The LORD will utterly exclude me from His people.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: And let the eunuch not say,
I am but a dry tree.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For this is what the LORD says:

To the eunuchs who keep My Sabbaths, who choose what pleases Me
and hold fast to My covenant—
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
I will give them, in My house and within My walls, a memorial and a name
better than that of sons and daughters. I will give them an everlasting namethat will not be cut off.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: And the foreigners who join themselves to the LORDto minister to Him, to love the name of the LORD, and to be His servants—all who keep the Sabbath without profaning it and who hold fast to My covenant—
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will bring them to My holy mountain and make them joyful in My house of prayer. Their burnt offerings and sacrificeswill be accepted on My altar, for My house will be called a house of prayer[^205] for all the nations.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Thus declares the Lord GOD, who gathers the dispersed of Israel:
I will gather to them still othersbesides those already gathered.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Come, all you beasts of the field; eat greedily, all you beasts of the forest.
Israel’s watchmen are blind, they are all oblivious; they are all mute dogs, they cannot bark; they are dreamers lying around, loving to slumber.

Like ravenous dogs, they are never satisfied. They are shepherds with no discernment; they all turn to their own way,
each one seeking his own gain:
Come, let me get the wine, let us imbibe the strong drink, and tomorrow will be like today, only far better!
segments.speaker: the Lord
